perturbation.original_text: CONTENT PROVIDER will have sole responsibility for providing, at its expense, the Content to COMPANY. CONTENT PROVIDER and COMPANY will determine mutually agreeable methods for the transmission and incorporation of updates to the Content.
perturbation.changed_text: CONTENT PROVIDER will have sole responsibility for providing, at its expense, the Content to COMPANY. CONTENT PROVIDER will utilize methods dictated by COMPANY for the transmission and incorporation of updates to the Content.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an internal contradiction by altering the responsibility for determining the methods of content transmission and updates. Initially, it was a mutual agreement; now, it's unilaterally dictated by COMPANY. This contradicts the initial agreement establishing a collaborative decision-making process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONTENT PROVIDER will have sole responsibility for providing, at its expense, the Content to COMPANY. CONTENT PROVIDER will utilize methods dictated by COMPANY for the transmission and incorporation of updates to the Content.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text specifies that the CONTENT PROVIDER and COMPANY mutually agree on methods for transmission and incorporation of content updates. The changed text stipulates that CONTENT PROVIDER will use methods dictated by COMPANY, removing the mutual agreement. This directly contradicts the initial collaborative approach, creating a clear inconsistency as stated in the explanation. This violates the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because there is a conflicting obligation.

perturbation.original_text: The Licensors shall have the right, upon reasonable notice, to inspect COMPANY'S books and records and all other documents and material in COMPANY'S possession or control with respect to the Content each has or might license to CONTENT PROVIDER which becomes the subject matter of this Agreement (and only with respect to Content each has or might license to CONTENT PROVIDER which become the subject matter hereof).
perturbation.changed_text: The Licensors shall have the right, upon reasonable notice not exceeding 24 hours, to inspect COMPANY'S books and records and all other documents and material in COMPANY'S possession or control with respect to the Content each has or might license to CONTENT PROVIDER which becomes the subject matter of this Agreement (and only with respect to Content each has or might license to CONTENT PROVIDER which become the subject matter hereof).
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the 'reasonable notice' to a specific timeframe ('not exceeding 24 hours'). This rigid timeline can be deemed unreasonable in some circumstances, potentially conflicting with the implicit understanding of 'reasonable' in the original clause. This rigid change can be very problematic for the 'COMPANY' considering the amount of documents that needs to be reviewed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: COMPANY will maintain accurate books and records with respect to the calculation of all payments due under this Agreement. The Licensors shall have the right, upon reasonable notice not exceeding 24 hours, to inspect COMPANY'S books and records and all other documents and material in COMPANY'S possession or control with respect to the Content each has or might license to CONTENT PROVIDER which becomes the subject matter of this Agreement (and only with respect to Content each has or might license to CONTENT PROVIDER which become the subject matter hereof).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'reasonable notice' to 'reasonable notice not exceeding 24 hours'. While this introduces a specific timeframe, it doesn't inherently contradict the contradicted text. Both sections now contain the same clause, and although the 24-hour limit could be argued as unreasonable in some situations, it's not a direct, undeniable contradiction that would automatically make the agreement unenforceable. It is still open to interpretation and does not necessarily prevent the parties from complying with both provisions.

perturbation.original_text: Each party hereby grants to the other a non-exclusive, limited royalty-free license to use its trademarks, service marks or trade names only as specifically described in this Agreement. All such use shall be in accordance with each party's reasonable policies regarding advertising and trademark usage as established from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party hereby grants to the other a non-exclusive, limited royalty-free license to use its trademarks, service marks or trade names only as specifically described in this Agreement. All such use shall be in accordance with policies dictated by COMPANY, overriding reasonable policies regarding advertising and trademark usage as established from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text gives COMPANY unilateral control over advertising and trademark usage policies, removing the 'reasonable' aspect and overriding policies established by either party. This creates a contradiction since previously each party policies were being respected and now is fully COMPANY that can override the other parties policies. Also creates unbalance in the power dynamics between the parties involved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereby grants to the other a non-exclusive, limited royalty-free license to use its trademarks, service marks or trade names only as specifically described in this Agreement. All such use shall be in accordance with policies dictated by COMPANY, overriding reasonable policies regarding advertising and trademark usage as established from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement that each party's 'reasonable' advertising and trademark usage policies were respected. The changed text gives COMPANY unilateral control, overriding the other party's policies. This creates a direct contradiction because the original agreement implied mutual respect for each party's policies, while the changed agreement allows COMPANY to dictate policies. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text but it represents the final state of the contract that has the contradiction.
